authoritative_requirement: Skill evaluation and behavioral maturity evidence must govern reusable behavior identity.
synthetic_mode_satisfaction: Pin the empty registry and prove no skill or maturity claim exists; any later skill requires exact evaluation evidence and a new immutable version.
test_seam: pin validation; nonempty and unsupported-maturity rejection; immutable receipt

authoritative_requirement: Maintain a versioned registry of reusable procedural capabilities with stable identities, contracts, authority, maturity, artifacts, and evaluation receipts.
synthetic_mode_satisfaction: Consume the explicit versioned empty registry and emit a snapshot that classifies five deterministic code-owned capabilities while containing zero skills.
test_seam: exact registry identity and hash; capability coverage; empty classification sets

authoritative_requirement: Organize canonical skills by authority lane without monolithic agents or nested skill hierarchies.
synthetic_mode_satisfaction: Preserve the authority-lane taxonomy and record that no canonical skill occupies a lane; code-owned capabilities remain explicitly code-owned.
test_seam: authority taxonomy; zero skill-lane assignments; undeclared owner rejection

authoritative_requirement: Track governed skill maturity and plasticity with promotion, regression, deprecation, and migration rules.
synthetic_mode_satisfaction: Preserve the maturity and plasticity taxonomies while proving that no skill entry or maturity claim exists in the synthetic registry.
test_seam: empty maturity/plasticity sets; claim-without-receipt rejection; changed-version identity

authoritative_requirement: Automatically detect canonical skill duplication, relation cycles, unsupported dependencies, and maturity violations.
synthetic_mode_satisfaction: Validate zero duplicates, zero skill dependencies, zero cycles, and zero unsupported maturity claims; fail closed if any are introduced.
test_seam: duplicate; dependency; cycle; maturity; deterministic validation tests
